title: BUEPT: C1 Inversion with Negative Adverbials
explanation: Advanced C1 practice focusing on Inversion with Negative Adverbials. Essential for BUEPT academic precision.

questions.q: He ran ___ of money.
I'll look ___ the matter.
Choose the correct: 'I have seen him yesterday.'
The plan was put ___ until next year.
He takes ___ his father.
A 'heavy smoker' is an example of a ___.
Break ___ means to start suddenly.
Carry ___ means to continue.
Get ___ with someone means to have a good relationship.
Let ___ someone means to disappoint them.
Analyze the following: 'Given the circumstances, he ___ have acted differently.'
Identify the error: 'The research, documenting by several experts, was comprehensive.'
Select the most academic form: 'The results ___ the hypothesis.'
Choose the correct transition: '___ the initial setbacks, the project was a success.'
Which sentence uses a passive voice correctly?
